title: 11 Herbal Creams For Inflamed Gums
intro_desc: Smokers often suffer from inflamed gums, also known as gum recession or gum disease. This happens because smoking reduces blood flow to the gums, making it harder for them to heal and fight off infection. As a result, smokers may experience pain, bleeding, and bad breath. Inflamed gums can also lead to tooth loss, which affects not only a person's self-esteem but also their ability to eat and speak properly. Smoking can also damage the bone that supports the teeth, making it harder to chew and digest food. This can lead to digestive problems, weight loss, and a lower quality of life. When inflamed gums go untreated, they can cause more serious health issues, such as heart disease and diabetes. Smokers who experience inflamed gums often struggle to find effective treatments, but one option is using herbal creams. These creams work by reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the gums. They contain natural ingredients that are absorbed quickly by the skin, providing relief from pain and discomfort. By applying these creams directly to the affected area, smokers can reduce their symptoms and promote healthy gums.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams contains soothing properties that can help reduce inflammation in gums. The gel extracted from the aloe vera plant is rich in vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals like calcium and potassium. It also contains anti-inflammatory compounds like aloe-emodin and aloin, which can help calm irritated gums and reduce swelling. These properties work together to create an environment that's conducive to healing and reducing pain. To use Aloe barbadensis cream for inflamed gums, gently apply a small amount to the affected area with your finger. Gently massage the cream into your gums using a circular motion, being careful not to press too hard. You can repeat this process 2-3 times a day, depending on the severity of your symptoms. It's also essential to maintain good oral hygiene by brushing and flossing your teeth regularly. When you start using Aloe barbadensis cream, you may notice a reduction in inflammation and swelling within a few days. Your gums may begin to feel less sensitive and tender, and you may experience a decrease in pain. In some cases, the cream may also help to reduce the risk of gum recession and promote healthy gum growth. However, if your symptoms persist or worsen, it's crucial to consult with your dentist or healthcare provider to rule out any underlying conditions that may require medical attention.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for inflamed gums

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams contains properties that help to soothe and calm inflamed gums. The main constituents of Echinacea purpurea creams include alkylamides, caffeic acid, and rosmarinic acid, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds help to reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums, making it easier to manage conditions such as gingivitis or periodontitis.  To use Echinacea purpurea creams for inflamed gums, you can apply a small amount directly to the affected area with a clean finger. Gently massage it in until it's fully absorbed, usually a few seconds. You can do this 2-3 times a day, or as needed. For best results, use a high-quality Echinacea purpurea cream that is free from harsh chemicals and artificial fragrances. When using Echinacea purpurea creams, you can expect to see a reduction in inflammation and swelling within a few days. Your gums may feel less tender and sensitive, making it easier to eat and drink without discomfort. If you have a bacterial infection, you may also see an improvement in the overall health of your gums. However, it's essential to consult with your dentist or healthcare provider before using Echinacea purpurea creams, especially if you have a severe condition or are taking medication.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for inflamed gums

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams contains a powerful compound called curcumin, which is known for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Curcumin helps to reduce swelling and pain in inflamed gums by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and preventing the growth of bacteria that can cause gum infections. This natural cream also contains other beneficial compounds such as polyphenols and flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that help to protect the gums from damage caused by free radicals. If you're experiencing inflamed gums, you can try applying a small amount of Curcuma longa cream to the affected area using a gentle circular motion. You can do this 2-3 times a day, preferably after brushing your teeth and before meals. To get the best results, be sure to massage the cream into your gums for about 30 seconds to allow the curcumin to penetrate deep into the tissues.  As you continue to use the cream, you may notice a reduction in swelling and pain, and your gums may start to feel softer and healthier. You may also notice an improvement in your breath and a reduction in bad taste caused by gum infections. In some cases, you may even be able to reduce the frequency of your dental visits, as the inflammation may subside and your gums may become healthier.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal creams for inflamed gums

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams contain a high concentration of tea tree oil, which is the main active ingredient responsible for its healing properties. This oil is rich in compounds called terpenes, particularly cineole, which has antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. When applied topically to inflamed gums, the tea tree oil in Melaleuca alternifolia creams helps to reduce swelling and pain by killing bacteria that can cause infections and inflammation.  To use Melaleuca alternifolia creams for inflamed gums, gently massage a small amount of the cream onto the affected area with your finger. Make sure to use a gentle touch to avoid irritating the gums further. You can repeat this process 2-3 times a day, or as needed. It's also essential to maintain good oral hygiene practices, such as brushing and flossing regularly, to prevent the buildup of bacteria that can cause gum inflammation.  Applying Melaleuca alternifolia creams to inflamed gums may help to reduce swelling and pain, promote healing, and prevent further infection. You may also notice an improvement in your overall oral health, with healthier gums and a reduced risk of complications such as abscesses or tooth loss. However, if your gum inflammation persists or worsens, it's crucial to consult with your dentist or healthcare provider for proper evaluation and treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal creams for inflamed gums

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. These properties help reduce swelling and fight off the bacteria that cause inflamed gums. The creams also contain antioxidants like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help protect the gums from further damage. If you're experiencing inflamed gums, apply a small amount of Zingiber officinale cream to the affected area using your finger or a cotton swab. Gently massage the cream into your gums for about 30 seconds. You can do this 2-3 times a day, or as needed. You can also mix the cream with a small amount of water to create a paste, and then apply it to your gums using a cotton swab. As the Zingiber officinale cream works to reduce inflammation and fight off bacteria, you may start to notice improvements in your gums within a few days. Your gums may become less red and swollen, and you may experience a reduction in pain and discomfort. You may also notice a decrease in bleeding when you brush or floss your teeth.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for inflamed gums

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams contains compounds like eucalyptol, a natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. These properties help reduce swelling and ease pain in inflamed gums. Eucalyptol also has antibacterial properties, which fight off infection-causing bacteria in the mouth. This can help prevent the spread of inflammation and promote healing. To use Eucalyptus globulus creams for inflamed gums, gently apply a thin layer to the affected area with a clean finger or cotton swab. Be sure to follow the product instructions for correct usage and concentration. You can apply the cream 2-3 times a day, ideally after brushing your teeth and rinsing your mouth with warm water. When using Eucalyptus globulus creams for inflamed gums, you can expect several outcomes. You may notice a reduction in swelling and pain, making it easier to eat and speak. Your gums may begin to heal faster, and the risk of infection decreases. However, if your symptoms persist or worsen, you should consult your dentist or healthcare provider for further guidance and treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams for inflamed gums

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis creams contains compounds like carnosic acid and ursolic acid, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. These properties help reduce swelling and fight off bacteria that can cause gum inflammation. When you apply Salvia officinalis creams to inflamed gums, the antioxidants in the cream, such as rosmarinic acid, work to calm the inflammation and promote healing.  To use Salvia officinalis creams for inflamed gums, gently massage a small amount onto the affected area with a clean finger or a cotton swab. You can do this 2-3 times a day, after brushing your teeth. Be sure to rinse your mouth thoroughly after application, and avoid eating or drinking for at least 30 minutes to allow the cream to be fully absorbed.  By following this routine, you may start to notice a reduction in gum inflammation and swelling within a few days. The antioxidants in the cream can help to heal the gums and prevent further irritation, promoting a healthy and pain-free mouth. In addition, the antimicrobial properties can help to prevent infections that can lead to more severe gum problems.
remedies.image_alt: Salvia officinalis herbal creams for inflamed gums

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams contains flavonoids and terpenoids, which are compounds that help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow. When applied to inflamed gums, these compounds work to combat swelling and redness, promoting a soothing effect. The flavonoids, specifically quercetin and kaempferol, have anti-inflammatory properties that help calm the gums, while the terpenoids, such as bilobalide, improve circulation, bringing oxygen and nutrients to the affected area. To use Ginkgo biloba cream for inflamed gums, apply a small amount directly to the affected area with a gentle touch, usually 2-3 times a day. You can also combine this with good oral hygiene practices, such as brushing and flossing, to maintain healthy gums. As you consistently use the cream, you may notice a reduction in swelling and redness, and your gums may feel more comfortable and less tender. This can also help prevent further inflammation and promote healing.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal creams for inflamed gums

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams contain anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help soothe and calm inflamed gums. The main constituents, including triterpenoid saponins and carotenoids, work together to reduce redness and swelling, while also fighting off bacteria and infections that can cause gum inflammation. To use Calendula officinalis cream for inflamed gums, gently apply a thin layer to the affected area with a clean finger or cotton swab, 2-3 times a day. Make sure to clean your hands before and after applying the cream, and avoid eating or drinking for about 30 minutes after application to minimize the risk of swallowing the cream. When you apply Calendula officinalis cream to your inflamed gums, you can expect to see a reduction in swelling and redness within a few hours. The cream may also help to reduce bleeding and gum tenderness. As the inflammation subsides, your gums may start to look healthier and more pinkish in color. However, it's essential to note that Calendula officinalis cream is not a replacement for regular dental care and professional cleaning. If your gum inflammation persists or worsens, you should consult your dentist or healthcare provider for proper evaluation and treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal creams for inflamed gums

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris creams contains thymol, a compound that has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. This is especially helpful for inflamed gums, as it can reduce swelling and fight off infections. Thymol is a natural antiseptic that can also help to soothe and calm irritated gums. When you apply Thymus vulgaris creams to inflamed gums, the thymol works to kill bacteria and reduce inflammation, which can help to prevent further irritation and promote healing. To use Thymus vulgaris creams for inflamed gums, gently apply a small amount to the affected area with a clean finger or cotton swab. You can do this 2-3 times a day, or as needed. Make sure to follow the instructions on the packaging and start with a small test area to check for any sensitivity.  When you apply Thymus vulgaris creams regularly, you can expect the inflammation and redness to subside, and the pain and discomfort to decrease. Your gums may start to look healthier and feel more comfortable, and you may notice a reduction in bleeding and sensitivity. It's also possible that the underlying cause of the inflammation, such as gum disease or poor oral hygiene, will be addressed, helping to prevent future episodes.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal creams for inflamed gums

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains licorice root extract, which is a natural anti-inflammatory that helps soothe and calm inflamed gums. The root contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has potent anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Glycyrrhizin helps reduce swelling, redness, and pain in the gums, making it an effective remedy for gum inflammation. Additionally, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams often include other soothing ingredients like aloe vera and tea tree oil, which further contribute to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. If you're experiencing inflamed gums, you can try using Glycyrrhiza glabra creams as a topical treatment. To get the most out of it, gently apply a thin layer of the cream to your affected gums using a cotton swab. Repeat this process 2-3 times a day, or as needed, for best results. It's also essential to maintain good oral hygiene by brushing your teeth regularly and flossing daily to prevent further irritation. Using Glycyrrhiza glabra creams regularly may lead to noticeable improvements in your gum health. You may start to experience a reduction in swelling and pain, as well as a decrease in inflammation. Your gums may appear healthier and more vibrant, with a reduction in redness and sensitivity. In some cases, you may even notice a decrease in bad breath or gum recession. However, if your symptoms persist or worsen, it's always best to consult with your dentist or healthcare provider for proper diagnosis and treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for inflamed gums

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent inflamed gums from forming. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and chamomile, have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and calm the gums. The antioxidants in these teas may also help reduce swelling and promote overall oral health. Regularly drinking herbal tea may be beneficial in maintaining healthy gums.
faq_safe: Herbal teas can be a good option to help with inflamed gums. However, it's essential to note that some herbs can be too strong if consumed daily. For example, peppermint tea can irritate gums if had in excess. It's best to drink herbal teas in moderation, like one cup a day, to see how your body reacts.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help reduce inflamed gums, but it takes time to see results. It may start to work within a few days to a week, but noticeable improvement often takes 2-4 weeks. You might notice a slight reduction in swelling and pain, and your gums may start to look healthier.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for inflamed gums is best done in the morning. This allows you to start the day with a soothing effect that can last for several hours. The anti-inflammatory properties of the tea can help calm swollen gums, making it easier to eat and speak throughout the day.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with some prescription medications for inflamed gums. For example, if you're taking blood thinners, certain teas like ginkgo biloba or garlic tea might increase their effects and cause bleeding. Also, teas like echinacea or licorice root may affect how your body reacts to antibiotics.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant, it's best to be cautious with herbal teas, especially if you have inflamed gums. Some herbs might not be safe for your baby. For example, sage and chamomile can stimulate the uterus, which isn't good during pregnancy. If you still want to try herbal teas, look for ones that are specifically labeled as pregnancy-safe.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas can be a soothing way to calm inflamed gums, but be cautious when breastfeeding. Some herbs like sage, peppermint, and chamomile are generally considered safe, but it's best to check the ingredients and avoid strong or concentrated brews. If your baby seems affected, try a different tea or reduce the amount consumed.
